bomList.description: 750V 300A IGBT module for 400V platform main drive
1200V 150A IGBT module for 800V platform main drive
750V 400A IGBT module for high-power commercial vehicle main drive

coreAdvantages.title: High Efficiency

coreAdvantages.title: Automotive Grade Reliability
coreAdvantages.description: All power modules are AEC-Q101 qualified, meeting ISO 26262 functional safety requirements

coreAdvantages.title: Full Platform Coverage

coreAdvantages.title: Mass Production Verified

faqs.question: How to select power modules for 400V and 800V platforms?
faqs.answer: For 400V platforms, we recommend 750V IGBT modules (such as BG300F08A13L5) to provide sufficient voltage margin. For 800V platforms, we recommend 1200V modules, with options for IGBT (BG150G12F13L4) or SiC (BM600F12B34U2). SiC offers higher efficiency but at a higher cost, while IGBT provides better cost-effectiveness.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team for module selection based on your power level, efficiency requirements, and cost budget.

faqs.question: What are the main differences between IGBT and SiC solutions?
faqs.answer: Main differences: (1) Efficiency: SiC is 0.5-1% higher. (2) Switching frequency: SiC supports 50-100kHz, IGBT is suitable for 10-20kHz. (3) System size: SiC reduces volume by 30-40%. (4) Cost: SiC has higher module cost. We recommend SiC for high-efficiency, high-power-density applications, and IGBT for cost-sensitive applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Evaluate the balance between efficiency gains and cost increase. Our FAE can provide ROI analysis.

faqs.question: How to design thermal management for power modules?
faqs.answer: Thermal management design points: (1) Heatsink thermal resistance <0.15K/W (IGBT) or <0.10K/W (SiC). (2) Phase change materials or high-performance thermal grease recommended. (3) Liquid cooling for high-power continuous operation. (4) Thermal simulation for worst-case verification. We provide thermal simulation models and design guidance.
faqs.decisionGuide: Provide your cooling conditions and operating requirements, our FAE can perform thermal calculations and heatsink selection.

faqs.question: How to design gate drive circuits?
faqs.answer: Gate drive design: (1) IGBT: +15V/-8V, gate resistor 5-15Ω. (2) SiC: +18V/-4V, gate resistor 2-8Ω. (3) Use isolated gate drivers with CMTI >100V/ns. (4) Gate loop inductance <10nH. (5) Active Miller clamp configuration. We provide recommended driver models and reference circuit designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE for gate drive reference design and PCB layout guidelines.

faqs.question: Do you provide reference designs and evaluation boards?
faqs.answer: Yes, we provide complete reference designs: (1) 75kW/400V reference design (based on BG300F08A13L5). (2) 150kW/800V reference design (based on BG150G12F13L4). (3) 200kW high-performance reference design (based on BM600F12B34U2). Includes schematics, BOM, PCB files, and control software.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact sales for evaluation boards and reference design materials.

faqs.question: What is the development cycle and technical support?
faqs.answer: Development cycle: (1) Evaluation phase: 2-4 weeks (using evaluation boards). (2) Prototype design: 4-8 weeks. (3) Testing and verification: 4-6 weeks. Technical support: (1) FAE on-site support. (2) Online technical support. (3) Thermal simulation and EMC analysis. (4) Failure analysis support.
faqs.decisionGuide: Provide your project schedule, we will develop a supporting technical support plan.

solutions.longDescription:
The New Energy Vehicle Motor Drive Solution from byd represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of byd components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
benefits: High efficiency: IGBT ≥98%, SiC ≥98.5%, improving driving range
High reliability: AEC-Q101 certified, automotive grade quality
Full series coverage: From 75kW to 200kW, passenger to commercial vehicles
Complete support: Reference designs, thermal simulation, and FAE technical support
Mass production verified: Large-scale application verification in BYD's own vehicles

customerCases.challenge: Developing 150kW/800V electric drive system requiring high efficiency and reliability
customerCases.solution: Adopted BG150G12F13L4 IGBT module with optimized gate drive and thermal management design
customerCases.results: System efficiency reached 98.2%, passed all automotive tests, now in mass production
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement and cost reduction with enhanced reliability.

customerCases.challenge: Developing 200kW/400V commercial vehicle electric drive requiring high power and overload capability
customerCases.solution: Adopted BG400F08A13L5 high-current IGBT module with liquid cooling system design
customerCases.results: Peak power 220kW, continuous power 200kW, meeting commercial vehicle heavy-load requirements
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement and cost reduction with enhanced reliability.

faeInsights.content: In new energy vehicle motor drive applications, the performance and reliability of BYD power modules have been fully verified. Key design points: (1) Select 750V or 1200V modules based on voltage platform. (2) Thermal design must have sufficient margin, especially for continuous high-power operation. (3) Gate drive design must be standardized to avoid false turn-on. (4) PCB layout must minimize parasitic inductance. Our team has extensive experience in electric drive system design and can provide full technical support to customers.
keyTakeaways: Select appropriate power module specifications based on voltage platform
Thermal design must fully consider continuous operation and worst-case scenarios
Gate drive and PCB layout are critical for reliability
Make full use of FAE resources to reduce development risks

steps: Calculate power requirements and select module current rating
Design thermal management system with adequate margin
Design gate drive circuit with proper protection
Optimize PCB layout for minimal parasitic inductance

solutions.name: Electric Vehicle Charging Station Solution
solutions.title: Electric Vehicle Charging Station Solution - BYD SiC High-Efficiency Fast Charging
solutions.description: Electric vehicle DC fast charging solution based on BYD SiC MOSFET modules, supporting 30kW-350kW power levels, achieving high-efficiency, high-power-density charging systems.

coreAdvantages.title: Ultra-High Efficiency
coreAdvantages.description: SiC technology achieves ≥99% peak efficiency, 1-2% higher than IGBT solutions, significantly reducing operating costs

coreAdvantages.title: High Power Density
coreAdvantages.description: High-frequency switching achieves 3kW/L+ power density, reducing charging station footprint

coreAdvantages.title: Wide Voltage Output
coreAdvantages.description: Supports 200V-1000V wide range output, compatible with various electric vehicles

coreAdvantages.title: Modular Design
coreAdvantages.description: 30kW standard power modules, flexible paralleling for different power levels

coreAdvantages.title: Complete Certification
coreAdvantages.description: Compliant with national, European, and American charging standards, supporting CCS/CHAdeMO/GB/T

faqs.question: How to select SiC modules for different power level charging stations?
faqs.answer: Power level and module selection: (1) 30-60kW: BM300F12B34U2. (2) 60-120kW: BM600F12B34U2. (3) 120-180kW: BM840F12B34U2. (4) 180-350kW: BM950F12B34U2. Multi-module paralleling is also possible, such as 2x BM600 for 240kW.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE for optimal selection based on target power level and cost budget.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of SiC charging stations compared to IGBT?
faqs.answer: SiC charging station advantages: (1) Higher efficiency: 99% vs 97%, saving approximately $5000/year in electricity for a single 120kW charger. (2) 3x higher power density, saving footprint. (3) Supports higher switching frequency, reducing magnetic component size. (4) Lower cooling requirements. (5) Lower cooling costs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate TCO (Total Cost of Ownership). SiC solutions typically recover the cost premium within 2-3 years.

faqs.question: How to design thermal management for charging stations?
faqs.answer: Thermal management solutions: (1) 30-60kW: Forced air cooling, heatsink thermal resistance <0.15K/W. (2) 60-120kW: Enhanced air cooling or liquid cooling. (3) 120kW+: Liquid cooling recommended, heatsink thermal resistance <0.08K/W. SiC has lower losses, cooling requirements are 30-40% lower than IGBT.
faqs.decisionGuide: Provide power level and environmental conditions, FAE can perform thermal simulation and heatsink design.

faqs.question: Is V2G (Vehicle-to-Grid) functionality supported?
faqs.answer: Yes, SiC module-based topology supports bidirectional power flow for V2G functionality. Requires bidirectional control algorithms and grid interface design. We provide bidirectional charging station reference designs supporting grid-connected and off-grid modes.
faqs.decisionGuide: V2G applications require additional grid certification. Contact FAE for detailed design solutions.

faqs.question: What are the EMC design considerations for charging stations?
faqs.answer: EMC design points: (1) SiC's high dv/dt requires special attention to EMI suppression. (2) Use shielded gate drive and common-mode filtering. (3) Proper PCB layout and grounding design. (4) Input/output filter design. We provide EMC optimization guidance and pre-testing support.
faqs.decisionGuide: EMC issues can be prevented at the design stage. Early design review with FAE is recommended.

faqs.question: Do you provide complete charging station solutions?
faqs.answer: We provide power module-level solutions including: (1) Power modules and drivers. (2) Power unit reference designs (30kW modules). (3) Control algorithms and communication protocol stacks. (4) Complete system integration guidance. Customers can quickly develop their own brand charging stations based on this.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact sales for charging station power unit reference design materials.

customerCases.challenge: Deploying 350kW ultra-fast charging requiring fast charging and compact design

customerCases.results: Peak power 350kW, 10-80% charging time <15 minutes, footprint reduced by 40%
customerCases.result: Achieved significant performance improvement and cost reduction with enhanced reliability.

faeInsights.content: The advantages of SiC technology in charging station applications are very significant. Key design experience: (1) Fully utilize SiC's high-frequency characteristics to reduce magnetic component size. (2) Pay attention to EMI issues caused by high dv/dt, ensure proper filtering and shielding. (3) Thermal design must have margin, especially for high-temperature summer environments. (4) Gate drive must be reliable, negative voltage turn-off is essential. Our reference designs have been fully verified and can help customers achieve fast time-to-market.
keyTakeaways: SiC's high-frequency characteristics can significantly reduce system size
High dv/dt requires attention to EMC design
Thermal design must consider worst-case environmental conditions
Reference designs can accelerate product time-to-market

steps: Determine target power level and voltage range
Select appropriate SiC module based on current requirements
Design thermal management system (air or liquid cooling)
Design gate drive with proper protection features

faqs.question: How to select power modules for different power level solar inverters?
faqs.answer: Selection recommendations: (1) 3-10kW residential: BG200I07N10H6 (three-level). (2) 10-50kW string: BG300F08A13L5. (3) 50-100kW: BG150G12F13L4. (4) 100-250kW: BM600F12B34U2 (SiC). Three-level topology is suitable for applications pursuing high efficiency.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE for selection based on power level, efficiency targets, and cost budget.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of three-level topology compared to two-level?
faqs.answer: Three-level topology advantages: (1) Switching losses reduced by 50%. (2) Better output voltage waveform quality, lower THD. (3) Reduced EMI, smaller filter size. (4) Efficiency improvement of 0.5-1%. BG200I07N10H6 is an I-Type module specifically designed for three-level topology.
faqs.decisionGuide: High-efficiency requirements (>98.5%) recommend three-level topology.

faqs.question: What are the special thermal design requirements for solar inverters?
faqs.answer: Thermal design requirements: (1) Outdoor applications must consider ambient temperatures above 45°C. (2) High altitude areas have reduced cooling capacity. (3) Dust environments require sealed designs. (4) Recommended thermal resistance: IGBT <0.15K/W, SiC <0.10K/W. We provide thermal design guidance for outdoor applications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Provide installation environment and altitude information, FAE can perform thermal simulation and design optimization.

faqs.question: What is the value of SiC in solar inverters?
faqs.answer: SiC value in solar: (1) Efficiency improvement of 0.5-1%, 25-year power generation increase of 2-4%. (2) Higher switching frequency, smaller magnetic components. (3) Fanless design becomes possible, improving reliability. (4) Particularly suitable for high-power and high-temperature environments.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate ROI of SiC solution based on 25-year power generation revenue increase vs cost increase.

faqs.question: Do you provide MPPT and grid-tie control algorithm support?
faqs.answer: Yes, we provide: (1) MPPT control algorithm reference code. (2) Grid synchronization and power control algorithms. (3) Anti-islanding protection implementation. (4) Grid adaptability design (low voltage ride-through, etc.). Helping customers quickly complete control software development.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE for control algorithm reference code and technical documentation.

faqs.question: Are the same power modules applicable to energy storage inverters?
faqs.answer: Yes, power modules are also applicable to energy storage inverters. Energy storage application characteristics: (1) Requires bidirectional power flow. (2) Frequent charge/discharge cycles, high reliability requirements. (3) SiC's high efficiency and long life are particularly suitable for energy storage. We provide energy storage inverter reference designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: Energy storage applications require bidirectional control and battery management. Contact FAE for energy storage solutions.

faeInsights.content: Solar inverter applications have extremely high requirements for efficiency and reliability. Key design experience: (1) Three-level topology has significant advantages in medium-to-high-power applications and is worth considering. (2) Thermal design must fully consider harsh outdoor environments. (3) MPPT algorithm efficiency directly affects power generation revenue and requires careful optimization. (4) Grid-tie control must meet various national grid standards. Our reference designs have been verified in multiple projects and can help customers quickly develop efficient inverters.

Outdoor thermal design must consider high temperature and dust environments
MPPT and grid-tie control algorithms require careful optimization
Reference designs can accelerate product development

Design thermal management for outdoor environment
Implement MPPT and grid-tie control algorithms

seoTitle: BYD Semiconductor Solutions | EV Motor Drive/EV Charging/Solar Inverter
seoDescription: Explore BYD Semiconductor application solutions: New Energy Vehicle Motor Drive, Electric Vehicle Charging Stations, Solar Inverters. Get BOM recommendations, design guidance, and technical support.

faqs.question: What application areas do BYD solutions cover?
faqs.answer: BYD Semiconductor solutions mainly cover: (1) New Energy Vehicles: Motor drive, OBC, DC-DC. (2) Charging Infrastructure: DC fast charging, V2G. (3) Renewable Energy: Solar inverters, energy storage PCS. (4) Industrial Applications: Variable frequency drives, UPS, welding machines. Our FAE team can provide professional support in all areas.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact FAE for detailed solution materials in your field.

faqs.question: How to obtain reference designs and evaluation boards?
faqs.answer: How to obtain: (1) Contact sales representative to apply for evaluation boards. (2) Sign NDA to obtain complete reference design materials (schematics, BOM, PCB). (3) FAE can provide on-site technical training and design review. (4) Some general design materials can be downloaded from the website.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact sales or FAE for evaluation boards and reference designs.

faqs.question: What technical support services does BYD provide?
faqs.answer: Technical support services include: (1) Product selection and application consulting. (2) Gate drive and thermal design guidance. (3) PCB layout review and optimization suggestions. (4) EMC problem diagnosis and solutions. (5) Failure analysis and improvement suggestions. (6) Reference designs and software algorithm support.
faqs.decisionGuide: FAE support is available at all project stages. Early involvement is recommended to reduce risks.

faqs.question: What is the typical development cycle for EV motor drive systems?
faqs.answer: Typical development cycle: (1) Concept and specification: 2-4 weeks. (2) Design and simulation: 4-6 weeks. (3) Prototype build and testing: 6-8 weeks. (4) Validation and certification: 8-12 weeks. Total cycle is typically 20-30 weeks depending on complexity and requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team early in your project to optimize the development timeline.

faqs.question: How does BYD ensure product quality and reliability?
faqs.answer: Quality assurance measures: (1) AEC-Q101 automotive certification for all power modules. (2) ISO 26262 functional safety compliance. (3) 100% testing and screening. (4) Extensive reliability testing including temperature cycling, HTRB, H3TRB. (5) Mass production verification in BYD's own vehicles. (6) Full traceability and quality documentation.
faqs.decisionGuide: Request qualification reports and reliability data from our sales team for your quality documentation.
